title: Montana Renewables to Expedite First Phase of MaxSAF Initiative
content: Calumet Inc. announced that its Montana Renewables biorefinery will achieve 120â€“150 million gallons per year (MMgy) of sustainable aviation fuel (SAF) capacity by early 2026, far earlier and at a fraction of the cost originally projected. Instead of the initially planned $150â€“250 million investment for half the SAF capacity by 2026, the company will invest just $20â€“30 million by enhancing existing assets and improving catalyst performance. This approach increases SAF yields from 2,000 to 8,000â€“10,000 barrels per day. The broader MaxSAF project, supported by a $1.44 billion DOE loan guarantee, still targets 300 MMgy SAF capacity by 2028. In Q1 2025, Montana Renewables posted $3.3 million in adjusted EBITDA (with tax attributes), a turnaround from a $13.4 million loss a year prior.
author: Analysts Account
